body.title: Consequence check: expert lenses + confidence system adoption
body.result: PARTIAL — 4 expert lenses in EXPERTS.md; implementation tickets (confidence column migration, habit telemetry gate) still pending; no premature breakage from confidence column absence observed yet
body.description:
**What changed:** Added 4 expert lenses to EXPERTS.md (Schmidhuber, Shankar, Willison, Cherny) and filed 6 implementation tickets for confidence-everywhere, universal LLM lineage, habit telemetry gate, and skill contract checking.

**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) confidence column migration on clan.memories could slow writes if index is missing; (2) habit telemetry gate could block legitimate habits if skill telemetry has thin data for newer code_ref functions; (3) pe_evaluate returning floats could make the threshold values load-bearing — wrong thresholds mean bad evaluations silently pass; (4) skill contract checker warnings could create noise if existing tooling adds tickets without the full template.

**Signals to watch:** habit write block rate in cc_inbox (should be < 5% of writes after 2 weeks); pe_evaluate confidence distribution in instance.llm_calls (should cluster near 0.1 or 0.9, not all 0.5 which means the evaluator is uncertain about everything); no migration-related slow query alerts.

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-04 or when T-confidence-schema closes.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket
